name: Tutorial 9: Hospital Readmissions Risk Analysis
description: PE diligence workflow for a 65-hospital multi-state portfolio using CMS HRRP data. Identifies penalty-carrying hospitals, surfaces a deteriorating health system, benchmarks against CMS national averages via two-column reference JOIN, and calculates penalty trend 2021 vs 2023.

narration.pre: Starting a hospital readmissions diligence session. A PE buyer is evaluating a multi-state hospital portfolio for HRRP penalty exposure.
narration.post: Session initialized. We will import facility-level readmissions data, load CMS national benchmarks, and run a structured penalty risk analysis.

narration.pre: Loading hospital readmissions data — 975 rows covering 65 hospitals across 5 states: Texas, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Georgia. Each row is one hospital-condition-year combination: the hospital's readmission rate for a specific CMS condition in a specific payment year, the national average rate, the Excess Readmission Ratio CMS uses to calculate penalties, and the estimated financial penalty. Three payment years: 2021, 2022, 2023. Five conditions: AMI, Heart Failure, Pneumonia, COPD, and Hip/Knee Replacement. This is facility-level quality reporting — the same data a PE buyer reviews when assessing readmission penalty exposure in a hospital acquisition.
narration.post: Dataset loaded: 975 rows, 14 columns. Facility-level CMS HRRP data across 65 hospitals, 5 states, 3 payment years, 5 conditions.

narration.pre: Loading the CMS HRRP national benchmark reference table — national average readmission rates by condition and payment year, the penalty threshold at an Excess Readmission Ratio of 1.0, and the 3% maximum penalty cap. We will JOIN this against the hospital data to identify which hospitals are above the national average and by how much.
narration.post: Benchmark reference loaded: 15 rows — 5 conditions across 3 payment years with CMS national averages and penalty parameters.

narration.pre: Inspect the schema before querying. Key columns: hospital_id, hospital_name, state, health_system, ownership_type, beds, condition, payment_year, readmission_rate_pct, national_avg_rate_pct, excess_readmission_ratio, penalty_pct, estimated_penalty_dollars. The excess_readmission_ratio is the CMS-calculated metric — values above 1.0 trigger a penalty. estimated_penalty_dollars is a proxy calculation. payment_year and condition together identify each row uniquely per hospital.
narration.post: Schema confirmed. 14 columns — hospital identifiers, quality metrics, and penalty calculations. Ready for analysis.

narration.pre: Let AW surface the initial insight cards — distribution of hospitals by state, condition breakdown, penalty rate across the portfolio. Use these as the entry point before drilling into specific risk findings.
narration.post: Insights generated from the HRRP schema. The penalty distribution and concentration cards provide the initial analytical direction.

details.question: Show me all hospitals with a penalty in 2023, ranked by total estimated penalty dollars

narration.pre: First analytical question: which hospitals are carrying the most penalty exposure in the most recent payment year? This is the starting point for any HRRP diligence.
narration.post: 61 of 65 hospitals are carrying a penalty in 2023. Grady Memorial in Georgia leads by total penalty, followed by Riverside Medical Center Houston. Three of the top entries are all Riverside Health System — a portfolio-level concentration of risk worth isolating.

narration.pre: The bar chart makes the Riverside concentration visible immediately. Click Chart.
narration.post: Three hospitals, same system, all in the top tier of penalty exposure. When you see a health system show up repeatedly in a penalty ranking, the next question is whether it is a trend or a snapshot.

details.sql:
SELECT payment_year, condition,
       ROUND(AVG(excess_readmission_ratio),4) AS avg_err,
       SUM(estimated_penalty_dollars) AS estimated_penalty
FROM dataset
WHERE health_system = 'Riverside Health System'
GROUP BY payment_year, condition
ORDER BY payment_year, condition
LIMIT 200

narration.pre: Drilling into Riverside Health System specifically — breaking down their Excess Readmission Ratio and estimated penalty by payment year and condition to see the trajectory.
narration.post: Riverside's Excess Readmission Ratio is getting worse every year. In 2021 their average ERR was 1.065. By 2023 it is 1.178 — accelerating. Total estimated penalty went from $36K in 2021 to $125K in 2023. A 3.5x increase in two years. In a PE acquisition, this is a value creation liability, not a one-time event.

narration.pre: The line chart shows the penalty trajectory. Click Chart.
narration.post: This is not noise — it is a directional trend. A buyer needs to model whether deterioration continues, stabilizes, or reverses, and build that assumption into the purchase price.

details.explanation: This query isolates Riverside Health System and breaks down their CMS Excess Readmission Ratio and estimated penalty by payment year and medical condition. The ERR measures how a hospital's readmission rate compares to the national expected rate — values above 1.0 mean the hospital readmits more patients than predicted. Riverside's ERR is increasing across most conditions each year, indicating a worsening quality trend that translates directly into growing financial penalties under the Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program.

narration.pre: Ask AW to explain what an Excess Readmission Ratio above 1.0 means in plain English — what does a 1.178 ERR translate to operationally and financially for a hospital system.
narration.post: Explain translates the CMS metrics into language a PE sponsor can read. ERR above 1.0 means more readmissions than expected — and growing ERR means the problem is getting worse, not better.

details.sql:
SELECT d.hospital_name, d.state, d.condition,
       d.readmission_rate_pct,
       r.national_avg_rate_pct,
       ROUND(d.readmission_rate_pct - r.national_avg_rate_pct, 2) AS rate_vs_national,
       d.excess_readmission_ratio,
       d.penalty_pct
FROM dataset d
LEFT JOIN reference r
  ON LOWER(d.condition) = LOWER(r.condition)
  AND d.payment_year = r.payment_year
WHERE d.payment_year = 2023
  AND d.readmission_rate_pct > r.national_avg_rate_pct
ORDER BY rate_vs_national DESC
LIMIT 200

narration.pre: Joining against the CMS benchmark reference. This uses a two-column JOIN — matching on both condition name and payment year — to compare each hospital's readmission rate to the correct national average for that condition and year.
narration.post: 189 hospital-condition combinations are above the national average in 2023. The top of the list is dominated by Riverside and Georgia hospitals. Riverside Houston's Hip/Knee Replacement is 2.73 percentage points above national average — the maximum 3.0% CMS penalty triggered on their highest-volume elective line.

details.sql:
SELECT health_system, state,
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2021 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) AS penalty_2021,
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2023 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) AS penalty_2023,
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2023 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) -
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2021 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) AS penalty_change
FROM dataset
GROUP BY health_system, state
HAVING penalty_2023 > 0 OR penalty_2021 > 0
ORDER BY penalty_change DESC
LIMIT 200

narration.pre: Comparing total penalty exposure by health system between 2021 and 2023. This surfaces which systems are getting worse and which are improving.
narration.post: Riverside's $89K penalty increase is the largest delta in the portfolio by a wide margin. Cleveland Clinic shows up with a $38K increase from zero — a system with no penalty in 2021 now carries one. That is the kind of deterioration a PE buyer needs to catch before close.

narration.pre: Saving this query as a reusable diligence template. Apply it to any hospital portfolio dataset and it surfaces the same deteriorating system risk pattern immediately.
narration.post: Saved. It now lives in Saved Queries — reusable across any hospital portfolio engagement.

narration.pre: Exporting the penalty trend comparison. This table goes directly into the diligence memo.
narration.post: Exporting the session passport. Four diligence findings documented: (1) Riverside Health System is a deteriorating asset — ERR up, penalty 3.5x in two years, root cause in Hip/Knee Replacement at the Houston flagship. (2) Georgia is the highest-risk state in this portfolio at 1.056 average ERR. (3) 61 of 65 hospitals carry some penalty in 2023 — portfolio-wide exposure. (4) The benchmark JOIN identifies 189 specific hospital-condition combinations to prioritize in clinical operations due diligence.

narration.pre: Wrapping up the session log to complete the audit trail.
narration.post: Analysis complete. HRRP readmissions diligence in AW follows the same pattern as Medicaid and Part D work — facility-level CMS data, national benchmark reference JOIN, natural language queries, reproducible session. Different dataset, identical methodology. Session summary: 1 dataset imported (975 rows), 1 reference table loaded (15 benchmarks), schema inspected, insights generated, 1 AI query, 3 manual SQL queries (including two-column reference JOIN), 2 charts, 1 explain, 1 query saved, 1 export.

resume_state.last_sql:
SELECT health_system, state,
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2021 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) AS penalty_2021,
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2023 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) AS penalty_2023,
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2023 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) -
       SUM(CASE WHEN payment_year=2021 THEN estimated_penalty_dollars ELSE 0 END) AS penalty_change
FROM dataset
GROUP BY health_system, state
HAVING penalty_2023 > 0 OR penalty_2021 > 0
ORDER BY penalty_change DESC
LIMIT 200
